question_text: True or False: Goodwill is amortized over its useful life under US GAAP.
explanation: Under US GAAP, goodwill is NOT amortized. Instead, it is tested for impairment at least annually. If the carrying value of a reporting unit exceeds its fair value, goodwill is written down (impaired). This is different from IFRS, which also does not amortize goodwill but uses a different impairment testing methodology. Note that other intangible assets with finite lives (like patents or customer relationships) ARE amortized under both GAAP and IFRS.
